metadata.description: Ferrumyx configuration backup created on 2026-05-01 05:12:43 UTC

additional_files.config/ferrumyx.example.toml:
# ferrumyx.toml — Ferrumyx configuration
# Copy this to ferrumyx.toml and edit for your environment.
# ferrumyx.toml is gitignored; never commit API keys.
#
# Quick start (API-only, no GPU needed):
#   1. Copy to ferrumyx.toml
#   2. Set llm.default_backend = "openai" (or "anthropic" / "gemini")
#   3. Set the api_key for your chosen provider
#   4. Set embedding.backend = "openai" and embedding.api_key

[database]
url            = "./data/lancedb"
max_connections = 10
min_connections = 2

# ── LLM ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[llm]
# Mode: "local_only" | "prefer_local" | "any"
# "local_only"   — only Ollama / local endpoints (no API keys needed)
# "prefer_local" — use local when available, fall back to remote for PUBLIC data
# "any"          — use the default_backend for all PUBLIC data (recommended for API-only)
mode = "prefer_local"

# Which backend to use for PUBLIC data (scientific literature, not patient data)
# Options: "openai" | "anthropic" | "gemini" | "openai_compatible" | "ollama"
default_backend = "ollama"

# Backend used for CONFIDENTIAL / INTERNAL data (must be local)
# Leave as "ollama" even if you don't have Ollama running — it simply won't be
# called unless you ingest data classified as INTERNAL or CONFIDENTIAL.
local_backend = "ollama"

# Allow INTERNAL-classified data to be sent to remote backends (with audit log)
# Default: false (safer). Set true only if you understand the privacy implications.
allow_internal_remote = false

# ── Provider: OpenAI ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[llm.openai]
# Get your key at https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
api_key  = ""                         # or set env var FERRUMYX_OPENAI_API_KEY
model    = "gpt-4o-mini"              # cheap + fast; use "gpt-4o" for higher quality
# Options: gpt-4o | gpt-4o-mini | gpt-4-turbo | o1 | o1-mini | o3-mini

# ── Provider: Anthropic ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[llm.anthropic]
# Get your key at https://console.anthropic.com/
api_key  = ""                         # or set env var FERRUMYX_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
model    = "claude-haiku-4-5"         # fastest + cheapest; use claude-sonnet for quality
# Options: claude-opus-4 | claude-sonnet-4-6 | claude-haiku-4-5

# ── Provider: Google Gemini ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
[llm.gemini]
# Get your key at https://aistudio.google.com/app/apikey
api_key  = ""                         # or set env var FERRUMYX_GEMINI_API_KEY
model    = "gemini-1.5-flash"         # free tier available; 1M context
# Options: gemini-1.5-pro | gemini-1.5-flash | gemini-2.0-flash | gemini-2.5-pro

# ── Provider: OpenAI-Compatible ──────────────────────────────────────────────
# Use for: Groq, TogetherAI, OpenRouter, LMStudio, vLLM, Mistral, etc.
[llm.openai_compatible]
base_url = "https://api.groq.com/openai"  # example: Groq
api_key  = ""                              # or env var FERRUMYX_COMPAT_API_KEY
model    = "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"      # change to match your provider's model
cached_chat = true                         # enable cache hints when provider/model supports it
# Other examples:
#   Groq:       base_url = "https://api.groq.com/openai"
#   OpenRouter: base_url = "https://openrouter.ai/api"
#   TogetherAI: base_url = "https://api.together.xyz"
#   LMStudio:   base_url = "http://localhost:1234"       api_key = ""
#   vLLM:       base_url = "http://localhost:8000"       api_key = ""
#   Mistral:    base_url = "https://api.mistral.ai"

# ── Provider: Ollama (local) ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
[llm.ollama]
base_url = "http://localhost:11434"
model    = "llama3.1:8b"
# GPU not required — quantised models run on CPU (slower but free)
# Recommended if compute is available: mistral-nemo, llama3.1:70b, biomistral

# ── Spending limits ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[llm.limits]
max_tokens_per_day_openai    = 500_000
max_tokens_per_day_anthropic = 500_000
max_tokens_per_day_gemini    = 1_000_000
max_cost_per_day_usd         = 20.0
alert_cost_threshold_usd     = 15.0

[llm.rate_limits]
openai_rpm     = 60
anthropic_rpm  = 40
gemini_rpm     = 60
compat_rpm     = 60
ollama_rpm     = 120

# ── Embedding ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[embedding]
# Which backend to use for text embeddings (written to paper_chunks.embedding)
# "rust_native"     — Microsoft BiomedBERT via Candle (recommended, no API key)
# "fastembed"       — ONNX Runtime via fastembed-rs (highest local CPU throughput; requires `ferrumyx-ingestion/fastembed_backend`)
# "openai"           — text-embedding-3-small (1536-dim, fast, cheap)
# "gemini"           — text-embedding-004 (768-dim, good quality, free tier)
# "ollama"           — local Ollama embedding model
backend         = "rust_native"
api_key         = ""         # only needed for openai/gemini
embedding_model = "microsoft/BiomedNLP-BiomedBERT-base-uncased-abstract-fulltext"
# Alternatives:
#   OpenAI:        backend = "openai", embedding_model = "text-embedding-3-small", embedding_dim = 1536
#   Gemini:        backend = "gemini", embedding_model = "text-embedding-004", embedding_dim = 768
#   Ollama:        backend = "ollama", embedding_model = "nomic-embed-text", embedding_dim = 768
#   FastEmbed:     backend = "fastembed", embedding_model = "BGEBaseENV15Q", embedding_dim = 768 (feature `ferrumyx-ingestion/fastembed_backend`)
embedding_dim   = 768        # BiomedBERT outputs 768-dim vectors

# ── Ingestion ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[ingestion]
# Default sources enabled for all ingestion jobs
sources = ["pubmed", "europepmc"]
# Full list: ["pubmed", "europepmc", "biorxiv", "medrxiv", "clinicaltrials", "crossref"]

[ingestion.pubmed]
# Optional NCBI API key for 10 req/s (vs 3 req/s without)
# Register free at https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/account/
api_key             = ""    # or env var FERRUMYX_PUBMED_API_KEY
requests_per_second = 3     # 10 with API key

[ingestion.europepmc]
requests_per_second = 5

[ingestion.crossref]
# CrossRef "polite pool" — set a valid email for higher rate limits
mailto              = "your@email.com"
requests_per_second = 5

[ingestion.performance]
# Validation mode: "off" | "audit" | "strict"
validation_mode = "audit"
# Chunk fingerprint scope: "paper" (recommended) | "global" | "off"
chunk_fingerprint_scope = "paper"
# Parse-quality guardrails for full text
pdf_parse_fallback_enabled = true
pdf_parse_min_sections = 2
pdf_parse_min_chars = 1200
pdf_parse_negative_revalidate_secs = 1800
# Relation extraction quality telemetry thresholds
predicate_coverage_min_unique = 6
predicate_coverage_max_generic_share = 0.55

# ── Scoring ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[scoring]
focus_cancer    = "PAAD"
focus_mutation  = "G12D"
primary_threshold   = 0.65
secondary_threshold = 0.45

# ── Knowledge Graph Rendering ────────────────────────────────────────────────
[graph]
default_mode = "2d"   # "2d" or "3d"
default_lens = "analysis" # "analysis" | "atlas" | "path"
render_preset = "analytical" # "analytical" or "dense"

[graph.mode_2d]
max_nodes = 260
max_links = 1000

[graph.mode_3d]
max_nodes = 200
max_links = 650

[graph.layout]
hub_suppression = 0.62
per_node_edge_cap = 12
atlas_max_clusters = 40

# ── NER service ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[ner]
# Default uses Rust-based Aho-Corasick trie matching (fast, no Docker needed)
# Options: "trie" (recommended) | "scispacy" (Docker Python service)
primary = "trie"
# service_url is only needed if primary = "scispacy"
# service_url = "http://localhost:8001"

# ── Structural analysis ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[structural]
alphafold_cache_dir = "./data/alphafold"
pdb_cache_dir       = "./data/pdb"
fpocket_binary      = "fpocket"   # must be on PATH

# ── Ranker Phase 4 ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[ranker.phase4]
# For cohorts above this size, ranker reads provider cache rows only and avoids
# live provider API fanout during interactive ranking.
provider_live_fetch_max_candidates = 8
source_backed_only = true
n3n4_semantic_fallback = false
structural_source_only = false

[ranker.phase4.structural]
prewarm_enabled = true
prewarm_max_genes = 8
cache_dir = "data/structural_cache"
fpocket_enabled = true
fpocket_bin = "fpocket"

[ranker.phase4.provider_refresh]
adaptive_enabled = true
base_interval_secs = 900
min_interval_secs = 180
max_interval_secs = 21600
stale_force_after_secs = 86400
backoff_factor = 2
success_accel_div = 2
recent_runs = 8

[ranker.phase4.background_refresh]
enabled = true
interval_secs = 900
max_genes = 24
batch_size = 6
retries = 1
fact_scan_limit = 4000
min_gene_mentions = 2
alert_error_rate = 0.55
alert_streak_runs = 3
cancer_code = ""   # Optional (e.g. "PAAD")
seed_genes = ""    # Optional CSV seed list (e.g. "KRAS,EGFR,BRAF")

[ranker.providers.cbioportal]
base_url = "https://www.cbioportal.org/api"
timeout_secs = 10
# api_token = ""

[ranker.providers.cosmic]
base_url = "https://cancer.sanger.ac.uk/cosmic/api"
timeout_secs = 10
mutation_data_path = ""
# api_key = ""

# ── Audit ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
[audit]
log_llm_calls    = true
log_ingestion    = true
log_kg_changes   = true
retain_days      = 365
